name: Ranch Round Steak
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the round steak into serving pieces, each about half an inch thick.
2. Trim excess fat from the meat and make shallow slashes in the edges to prevent curling during cooking.
3. In a bowl, combine flour, dry mustard, salt, and pepper. Use this mixture to coat the steak on all sides.
4. Reserve some of the seasoned flour mixture for later use.
5. In a skillet, brown the meat in hot shortening over medium-high heat. Work in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding the pan.
6. Once the meat is browned on both sides, push it aside and stir together the reserved flour mixture with water and Worcestershire sauce in the same skillet.
7. Return the meat to the skillet and continue cooking until a gravy forms. Stir occasionally to prevent burning.
8. Reduce heat as needed and simmer for 1 to 1 1/4 hours or until the steak is tender.
9. Remove the cooked steak from the pan and let it rest on a platter. Skim any excess fat from the gravy in the pan.
10. Drizzle the gravy over the steak before serving.
